description:
# Why

The integration-test helpers that launch the `bl` binary ‚Äî `tests/common::bl`, `tests/common::bl_as`, and the `bl_fresh` closure in `tests/init.rs` ‚Äî each loop `GIT_ENV_VARS` and `env_remove` it from the `assert_cmd::Command` before running `bl`.

That scrub does nothing. It defends the wrong layer:

- `bl` is already immune to an inherited `GIT_*` environment. It discovers the repo via `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` and runs every git subprocess through `git::clean_git_command`, all of which scrub `GIT_ENV_VARS` themselves (the production path, unchanged by bl-d1db). Scrubbing the env of an already-immune binary buys nothing.
- And in practice `GIT_*` is not even set when the suite runs: the pre-commit hook `unset`s the git-local env before `cargo test`, and a plain `cargo test` / `make check` never had it. The loop removes vars that are absent.

So it is cargo-cult: it reads as if test isolation depends on it when it does not, and it obscures that the real guarantee lives inside `bl`.

# Scope

Remove the `for var in GIT_ENV_VARS { c.env_remove(var) }` loop from the three `Command::cargo_bin(\"bl\")` builders:

- `tests/common/mod.rs` ‚Äî `bl()` and `bl_as()`
- `tests/init.rs` ‚Äî the `bl_fresh` closure

# Out of scope / keep

`GIT_ENV_VARS` itself stays. `tests/init.rs` still has three *raw* `git` `StdCommand` calls (one `git init`, two `config --unset`) that genuinely need the scrub ‚Äî real `git`, not the `bl` binary. A fuller tidy could route those through `git::clean_git_command` too and then retire the `GIT_ENV_VARS` re-export entirely; reasonable, but a separate call ‚Äî do not pull it into this ball.

# Origin

Spotted while delivering bl-d1db (test git-helper consolidation, c3d7b78 on main): bl-d1db rebuilt `tests/common`'s git helpers on `clean_git_command` and re-exported `GIT_ENV_VARS`, which made this dead scrub visible.
title: Drop dead GIT_* scrub before the bl binary in tests
